title: Testing, Release Gates, and Memory Incident Defense · C Memory Workbench
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working production memory-safety defense dossier while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of production memory-safety defense dossier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, ABI, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk.
Test supported GCC and Clang versions, optimization modes, architectures, libraries, feature macros, and warning baselines.
Pin source, dependencies, compiler, flags, generated files, symbols, SBOM, provenance, and artifact identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Memory Test Layers
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 6573. Produce bounded C evidence to decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Memory Test Layers.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, abi, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Memory Test Layers, keep Memory Test Layers connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Memory Incident Response
steps.instruction:
For this workshop demonstration of cmem-memory-incident-response, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-memory-incident-response-154s5v */" for changed case 2857. It must decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
